hcd_data.statement_of_contribution: The property has value as a nineteenth-century House-form building that reflects vernacular interpretations of Victorian architectural styles. Through its construction date and typology, the property is associated with the District’s early residential development from the 1850s to the early 1900s. The property retains many of its original building features including its cross-gable roofline, fenestration pattern, and double- height projecting bay window. The property has contextual value because it supports and maintains the historic mixed use character of the area and is physically, functionally, visually, and historically linked to the surrounding market area. Summary: Reference Primary Address Entrance Address(es) Number 30. 264 Augusta Avenue 268 Augusta Avenue 34 DCH | FO :D 270, 272, & 274 Augusta Avenue (L-R) 270, 272, & 274 AUGUSTA AVENUE

facade_description: 268 Augusta Ave presents a bay-and-gable domestic facade within the Kensington Market HCD. The street elevation spans roughly 5.2 metres across 2 storeys and is capped by a cross-gable, consistent with its semi-detached bay-and-gable house-form typology. The facade reads primarily as mixed in a cream, orange, and rust storefront with white-framed glazing and orange patio railings palette, laid in running bond with light grey mortar joints. The facade keeps a domestic opening rhythm of 1, 2 window bays from lower to upper levels, using mixed, and 1 principal entrance. Character-defining facade elements include cornice. HCD note: The property has value as a nineteenth-century House-form building that reflects vernacular interpretations of Victorian architectural styles. Through its construction date and typology, the property is associated with the District’s early residential development from the 1850s to the early 1900s. The property retains many of its original building features including its cross-gable roofline, fenestration pattern, and double- height projecting bay window. The property has contextual value because it supports and maintains the historic mixed use character of the area and is physically, functionally, visually, and historically linked to the surrounding market area. Summary: Reference Primary Address Entrance Address(es) Number 30. 264 Augusta Avenue 268 Augusta Avenue 34 DCH | FO :D 270, 272, & 274 Augusta Avenue (L-R) 270, 272, & 274 AUGUSTA AVENUE
